task_completion.description: Verify that the YDBGui web interface is open and accessible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displaying the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has searched for or selected the target global.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text '^AUPNVSIT' is visible in the search bar, navigation input, or global list of the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the ^AUPNVSIT global has been successfully navigated to.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewing area of YDBGui displays the contents of the ^AUPNVSIT global, showing its structure or a list of entries.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least three visit entries are being browsed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface shows at least three different numeric IENs (Internal Entry Numbers) under the ^AUPNVSIT global that have been expanded, selected, or are actively being viewed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the zero-node data for the visit entries is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen displays the zero-node data (often shown as a '0' subscript or a delimited string containing date/time, patient link, and service category) for at least three different visit entries.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the YDBGui web interface rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The global data is displayed within the YDBGui web interface in a browser, not in a terminal emulator using MUMPS commands.

integrity.description: Verify that the data appears to be genuine VistA global data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data follows standard VistA global structures (e.g., delimited strings with care details) and is viewed within the actual application, not a text editor or mock file.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not simply type the expected output.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent typing the task description or expected global names into a blank document or text file to simulate completion.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target interface is YDBGui. The target global is ^AUPNVSIT, which corresponds to VistA File #9000010 (VISIT). The agent must display the zero-node data for at least 3 different visit entries (IENs) under this global in the YDBGui interface.
